title: Extensions of Globally Valued Fields and Arithmetic Geometry
speaker: Michal Szachniewicz
abstract: Globally valued fields form a generalisation of global fields that fits into the context of first order (continuous) logic. I will describe these structures, and outline how they are connected to various parts of arithmetic geometry: Arakelov geometry, heights, lattices and adeles. Afterwards, I will present some results and questions, regarding extensions of globally valued fields.

proof_stages.label: Model-theoretic template for fields with extra structure
proof_stages.summary: The talk motivates globally valued fields by reviewing how fields with derivations, orders, automorphisms, and valuations have existentially closed subclasses and associated geometries, then asks for the corresponding class and geometry in the global-height setting.

proof_stages.label: Height-constrained Diophantine problems
proof_stages.summary: The central class of problems is formulated as polynomial equations together with inequalities or equalities involving heights, with elementary height identities giving the apparent obstructions and a 2023 theorem asserting that these obstructions are complete over the algebraic numbers.

proof_stages.label: Globally valued fields and existential closure of Qbar
proof_stages.summary: A globally valued field is recast as a field equipped with global height functions satisfying the stated axioms, so that the theorem says Qbar with its standard height is existentially closed and motivates the problem of describing all GBF extensions.

proof_stages.label: Valuative and measure-theoretic classification of GBF structures
proof_stages.summary: A joint theorem with Ben Yaacov identifies GBF structures on a countable field with equivalence classes of L1-valued valuations satisfying a product formula, equivalently with product-formula measures on the space of absolute values.

proof_stages.label: Arakelov-geometric description of function-field extensions
proof_stages.summary: For the function field of a variety, a GBF extension is interpreted via heights of rational maps to projective spaces and then identified with a positive homomorphism on a completed group of Arakelov b-divisors or metrized line bundles, with intersection products supplying dense natural functionals.

proof_stages.label: Necessary properties of existentially closed GBFs
proof_stages.summary: Existentially closed GBFs are shown to be algebraically closed, and a known theorem says that the valuation image of k* is dense in the kernel of the integral, which can be rephrased adelically as an approximate Picard-type uniformization.

proof_stages.label: Adelic-vector-space criterion and global analytic geometry program
proof_stages.summary: The speaker proposes describing existentially closed GBFs by the absence, up to approximation, of nontrivial finite-dimensional adelic vector spaces, relates this to natural structures from arithmetic varieties, and ends with open questions and proof sketches in the Q&A.
